title: Genealogy, Joseph, and the virgin birth
summary_text: The book opens with Jesus Christ’s genealogy from Abraham through David to Joseph, highlighting women such as Tamar, Rahab, Ruth, and Bathsheba’s story. Mary, engaged to Joseph, is found with child by the Holy Spirit; an angel tells Joseph in a dream not to fear to take her as wife, for the child will save his people and fulfill Immanuel. Joseph obeys, names him Jesus at birth, and keeps her as his wife without knowing her intimately until she bore her firstborn son.
themes: Son of David, son of Abraham
key_points: The genealogy frames Jesus as heir of Abraham and David.
Joseph planned to put Mary away secretly until the angel intervened.
The child is from the Holy Spirit and will save his people from their sins.
A virgin shall conceive—God with us.
Joseph named him Jesus as commanded.
evidence_phrases: The book of the genealogy of Jesus Christ
Joseph, her husband, being a righteous man
she was found with child by the Holy Spirit
you shall call his name Jesus
they shall call his name Immanuel

title: Magi, Herod, Egypt, and Nazareth
summary_text: Magi from the east come to Jerusalem seeking the born King of the Jews; Herod and Jerusalem are troubled. Priests and scribes point to Bethlehem; the star leads the magi to the child, and they worship with gifts. Warned in dreams, they avoid Herod, Joseph flees to Egypt with Mary and Jesus, and Herod slaughters the infants in Bethlehem. After Herod’s death another dream sends the family to Galilee, and they settle in Nazareth to fulfill the prophets.
key_points: Wise men sought the newborn king of the Jews.
Micah’s Bethlehem is cited as Messiah’s birthplace.
Joseph fled to Egypt until Herod died.
Herod’s massacre fulfilled Rachel weeping at Ramah.
Jesus was called a Nazarene in Galilee.
evidence_phrases: Where is he who is born King of the Jews?
You, Bethlehem, land of Judah
Rachel weeping for her children
out of Egypt I called my son
He came and lived in a city called Nazareth

title: John the Baptist and Jesus’ baptism
summary_text: John preaches repentance in the wilderness, baptizing in the Jordan as crowds confess sins; he warns religious leaders they cannot rely on ancestry and calls for fruit worthy of repentance, announcing one mightier whose sandals he is unworthy to carry, who will baptize with the Holy Spirit and fire. Jesus comes to be baptized; the heavens open, the Spirit descends like a dove, and the voice declares this is God’s beloved Son in whom he is well pleased.
key_points: John’s baptism was for repentance in the Jordan wilderness.
He demanded fruit, not pride in Abraham as father.
He pointed to the stronger one who would baptize with the Spirit and fire.
Jesus insisted on fulfilling all righteousness at his baptism.
The Father’s voice affirmed Jesus as his beloved Son.
evidence_phrases: Repent, for the Kingdom of Heaven is at hand!
You offspring of vipers
I indeed baptize you in water
he will baptize you in the Holy Spirit
This is my beloved Son, with whom I am well pleased

title: Temptation, Galilee, and first disciples
summary_text: The Spirit leads Jesus into the wilderness forty days where the devil tempts him with bread, temple pinnacle, and kingdoms; Jesus answers each from Scripture. John’s arrest moves Jesus to Capernaum in Galilee of the Gentiles, fulfilling light-dawning prophecy. He calls Simon Peter, Andrew, James, and John from their nets to follow him, and his fame spreads as he teaches, preaches the good news of the Kingdom, and heals every disease and sickness among the people.
key_points: Jesus resisted temptation by quoting Deuteronomy.
He began preaching repentance because the Kingdom of Heaven was at hand.
His move to Capernaum fulfilled Isaiah’s prophecy about Zebulun and Naphtali.
He called disciples to fish for people.
He healed the sick and his report spread through Syria.
evidence_phrases: Man shall not live by bread alone
Repent, for the Kingdom of Heaven is at hand
The people who sat in darkness saw a great light
Follow me, and I will make you fishers for men
healing all kinds of sickness

title: Sermon on the Mount: Beatitudes and the law’s depth
summary_text: Jesus teaches the crowds on the mountain: the Beatitudes bless the poor in spirit, mourners, meek, hungry for righteousness, merciful, pure, peacemakers, and persecuted. He calls disciples salt and light, warns against law’s least commands, and intensifies the law’s heart—anger, lust, divorce, oaths, retaliation—commanding love for enemies and prayer for persecutors, summoning perfection like the heavenly Father.
key_points: The blessed are those who hunger for righteousness and are persecuted for righteousness’ sake.
Disciples are salt of the earth and light of the world.
Jesus came to fulfill the law and prophets, not destroy them.
Anger and lust are judged at the heart level.
Love your enemies and pray for those who persecute you.
evidence_phrases: Blessed are the poor in spirit
You are the salt of the earth
one jot or one tittle
everyone who is angry with his brother
whoever looks at a woman to lust after her
love your enemies
You shall therefore be perfect

title: Prayer, fasting, treasure, and anxiety
summary_text: Jesus warns against practicing righteousness before people to be seen, teaches the Lord’s Prayer with God as Father in secret, and forbids vain repetition. Fasting and treasure in heaven replace earthly show; the eye as lamp and serving God alone oppose mammon. He forbids anxiety about life, food, and clothing, pointing to birds and lilies and urging seeking first God’s Kingdom and righteousness with tomorrow left to God.
key_points: Give, pray, and fast for God who sees in secret.
The model prayer hallows God’s name and seeks daily bread and forgiveness.
Forgiving others is tied to receiving forgiveness.
You cannot serve God and mammon.
Seek first the Kingdom and his righteousness.
evidence_phrases: your Father who sees in secret
Our Father in heaven
forgive us our debts
no one can serve two masters
don’t be anxious for your life
Seek first God’s Kingdom

title: Judging, asking, and the two foundations
summary_text: Jesus forbids hypocritical judgment with the speck and beam, commands holy things to the holy and persistence in asking, seeking, and knocking, and states the golden rule sums the law and prophets. The narrow gate, false prophets as wolves in sheep’s clothing known by fruit, and lip-service lords who do not obey end with the wise man who builds on the rock versus the foolish on sand when storms come.
key_points: Take the beam from your own eye first.
Keep asking, seeking, and knocking; the Father gives good gifts.
Do to others what you would have them do to you.
Many will say Lord Lord yet not enter.
Hearing and doing builds on the rock.
evidence_phrases: don’t judge, so that you won’t be judged
Ask, and it will be given you
whatever you desire for people to do to you
Enter in by the narrow gate
by their fruits you will know them
like a wise man who built his house on a rock

title: Healing the sick and calming the storm
summary_text: Jesus cleanses a leper, heals a centurion’s servant at a word marveling at Gentile faith, heals Peter’s mother-in-law and many demoniacs and sick that evening, and stills a storm the disciples feared would drown them. On the other side he meets fierce demoniacs; the demons enter a herd of swine that rush into the sea, and the town asks him to leave while the healed man is sent to proclaim what the Lord did.
key_points: Jesus touched the leper and he was cleansed.
The centurion’s faith exceeded what Jesus found in Israel.
Jesus rebuked the wind and the sea became calm.
Demons named Legion begged to enter swine.
The delivered man was told to show his friends what the Lord had done.
evidence_phrases: I am willing. Be cleansed
I haven’t found so great a faith
Why are you fearful, you of little faith?
What is it to us, Jesus, Son of God?
Go to your friends, and tell them what great things the Lord has done

title: Forgiveness, calling Matthew, and harvest workers
summary_text: Jesus forgives and heals a paralytic carried through the roof, proving Son of Man authority on earth to forgive sins, and calls Matthew from the tax office, eating with sinners as physicians for the sick. A ruler’s daughter and a woman with bleeding are healed; blind men and a mute demoniac are restored, yet Pharisees blaspheme the Spirit. Jesus has compassion on harassed crowds like sheep without a shepherd and tells disciples to pray for laborers for a plentiful harvest.
key_points: The paralytic walked to show the Son of Man forgives sins on earth.
Jesus ate with tax collectors and sinners.
He raised a ruler’s daughter saying she was not dead but sleeping.
The woman touched his garment and was healed by faith.
The harvest is plentiful but the laborers are few.
evidence_phrases: your sins are forgiven
Follow me
I desire mercy, and not sacrifice
the girl arose
your faith has made you well
the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins
pray that the Lord of the harvest will send out laborers

title: The Twelve sent with warnings
summary_text: Jesus names the twelve apostles and sends them to Israel’s lost sheep with authority over unclean spirits and disease, instructing them on provision, peace, shaking dust, sheep among wolves, Spirit-given words before courts, loving God more than family, taking up the cross, losing life to find it, and receiving prophets and little ones. He promises a prophet’s reward and that even a cup of cold water will not lose its reward.
key_points: The twelve were given power to heal and cast out demons.
They were sent only to the lost sheep of Israel at this stage.
They would face councils and scourging but the Spirit would speak.
Whoever denies Jesus before others will be denied before the Father.
Whoever gives a cup of cold water will not lose reward.
evidence_phrases: these twelve Jesus sent out
lost sheep of the house of Israel
I send you out as sheep among wolves
don’t be afraid of those who kill the body
Whoever denies me before men
whoever gives one of these little ones just a cup of cold water

title: John’s question and rest for the weary
summary_text: John’s disciples ask if Jesus is the coming one; Jesus points to his healings and good news to the poor as Isaiah’s signs. He honors John as more than a prophet yet least in the Kingdom is greater than he. Chorazin, Bethsaida, and Capernaum are warned for unrepentance; the Father hides things from the wise and reveals them to infants. Jesus invites the weary to take his yoke and learn gentleness for rest.
themes: John the greatest born of women
key_points: Jesus answered John with deeds matching messianic prophecy.
John was Elijah who was to come for those with ears to hear.
Tyre and Sidon would have repented more than Galilean towns.
The Father has entrusted all things to the Son.
Come to me all who labor and are heavily burdened.
evidence_phrases: the dead are raised up
Among those who are born of women
if the mighty works had been done in Tyre and Sidom
no one knows the Son, except the Father
Come to me, all you who labor
you will find rest for your souls

title: Lord of the Sabbath and the sign of Jonah
summary_text: Jesus defends eating grain and healing on the Sabbath as mercy over sacrifice; Pharisees plot to destroy him. He heals a withered hand, withdraws, and warns against blasphemy of the Spirit while all sin and speech against the Son may be forgiven. He refuses a sign except Jonah three days in the great fish as Son of Man three days in the earth. When told his mother and brothers seek him, he defines family as whoever does God’s will.
key_points: The Son of Man is Lord of the Sabbath.
Blasphemy against the Spirit will not be forgiven.
Jonah’s sign prefigured Jesus’s burial and resurrection.
Nineveh and the queen of the south will judge this generation.
Whoever does the will of God is Jesus’s brother and sister and mother.
evidence_phrases: it is lawful to do good on the Sabbath
the Son of Man is Lord of even the Sabbath
whoever speaks against the Holy Spirit
no sign will be given … but the sign of Jonah
whoever does the will of my Father

title: Parables of the Kingdom
summary_text: Jesus teaches by the sea: the sower, weeds among wheat, mustard seed, and leaven, explaining to disciples why he speaks in parables and interpreting the sower and weeds. He compares the Kingdom to treasure, a pearl, and a net sorting fish, and asks if scribes understand old and new treasure. At Nazareth he is amazed at unbelief and does few mighty works.
key_points: The word meets path, rock, thorns, and good soil.
An enemy sowed weeds; wait until harvest to separate.
The Kingdom starts small yet fills the earth.
The net gathers all kinds for final sorting.
Jesus marveled at their unbelief in his hometown.
evidence_phrases: A sower went out to sow
An enemy has done this
a grain of mustard seed
leaven … hid in three measures of meal
the kingdom of heaven is like a treasure
A prophet is not without honor, except in his own country

title: John’s death, feeding five thousand, walking on water
summary_text: Herod fears Jesus is John raised; the narrative recounts John’s rebuke of Herod’s marriage, imprisonment, Salome’s dance, and John’s beheading. Jesus withdraws and feeds five thousand with five loaves and two fish, then sends disciples ahead while he prays. He walks on the sea; Peter sinks when doubting but is caught; they worship him as God’s Son.
key_points: Herod executed John though he feared the crowd.
Jesus healed the sick and fed the multitude with twelve baskets left over.
He went alone to pray on the mountain.
Peter walked on water until he saw the wind.
Those in the boat worshiped him as the Son of God.
evidence_phrases: This is John the Baptizer
Bring them here to me
five thousand men
in the fourth watch of the night
Lord, save me!
You of little faith, why did you doubt?
Truly you are the Son of God

title: Traditions, the Canaanite woman, and four thousand fed
summary_text: Pharisees challenge unwashed hands; Jesus teaches that what defiles comes from the heart—evil thoughts, murder, adultery—not unwashed hands. A Canaanite woman’s faith wins healing for her daughter despite initial silence and the children’s bread metaphor. Jesus feeds four thousand in the wilderness with seven loaves and gathers seven baskets of fragments.
key_points: Isaiah’s prophecy applied to lip-honor void of heart.
Evil from within defiles, not food without ceremonial washing.
The woman’s persistence led Jesus to praise her faith.
Her daughter was healed instantly.
Four thousand were fed with abundant leftovers.
evidence_phrases: This people honors me with their lips
the things which proceed out of the mouth
even the dogs eat the crumbs
woman, great is your faith
They all ate and were filled

title: Peter’s confession and taking up the cross
summary_text: Pharisees and Sadducees seek a sign; Jesus points to weather signs yet refuses their evil generation except Jonah. Disciples forget bread; Jesus warns of leaven of Pharisees and Sadducees as teaching. At Caesarea Philippi Peter confesses Jesus as Christ; Jesus blesses him, promises keys of the Kingdom, and foretells his suffering, death, and resurrection. He calls disciples to deny self, take the cross, and lose life to save it, promising some will see the Son of Man coming in glory before they taste death.
themes: Christ, Son of the living God
key_points: Peter received revelation that Jesus is the Christ.
Jesus began to show he must suffer, die, and rise the third day.
Peter rebuked him and was called Satan for minding human things.
Taking up the cross follows Jesus for life found in losing it.
Some standing there would see the Son of Man coming in his Kingdom.
evidence_phrases: no sign will be given … but the sign of Jonah
You are the Christ, the Son of the living God
on this rock I will build my assembly
Get behind me, Satan!
If anyone desires to come after me, let him deny himself
there are some standing here who will in no way taste of death

title: Transfiguration, boy healed, temple tax
summary_text: Six days later Jesus is transfigured before Peter, James, and John with Moses and Elijah; the Father commands listening to his Son. Coming down, they learn Elijah came in John. Jesus heals a boy the disciples could not cast out, teaching prayer and fasting and faith as a mustard seed. In Capernaum he pays the temple tax from a coin in a fish’s mouth to avoid offense.
key_points: Jesus shone like the sun on the mountain.
A voice said this is my beloved Son; hear him.
John the Baptist fulfilled Elijah’s coming in a sense.
The boy’s demon left after Jesus rebuked it.
The coin in the fish’s mouth paid temple tax for Peter and Jesus.
evidence_phrases: he was transfigured before them
This is my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased. Listen to him.
Elijah indeed comes first
if you have faith as a grain of mustard seed
cast the demon out of him
give to them for me and you

title: Greatness, discipline, and forgiveness
summary_text: Jesus says the greatest must become like a little child; millstones await those who cause little ones to stumble. Cut off hand or eye if it makes you stumble. The shepherd seeks one lost sheep. If a brother sins, go alone then with witnesses then the assembly; binding and loosing in agreed prayer. Peter asks forgiveness limits; Jesus answers seventy times seven with the parable of the unforgiving servant whose lord forgave a huge debt yet he choked a fellow servant.
key_points: Whoever humbles like a child is greatest in the Kingdom.
Woe to the world’s stumbling traps.
The Father does not desire one little one to perish.
Two or three gathered agree in prayer are heard.
The unforgiving servant was delivered to tormentors until he paid all.
evidence_phrases: unless you turn and become as little children
woe to that person through whom the stumbling block comes
it is not desirable that one of these little ones should perish
if two of you will agree on earth
seventy times seven
forgive your brother from your hearts

title: Marriage, children, and the rich young man
summary_text: Jesus teaches God’s creation design for marriage against hard-hearted divorce; disciples marvel at marriage’s bond. He blesses little children, saying the Kingdom belongs to such. A rich young ruler asks eternal life; Jesus tests him with commandments and selling all to follow; the man leaves sorrowful. Jesus says riches make salvation hard yet with God all things are possible. Peter’s leaving all is met with promise of renewal and inheritance, with many first last and last first.
key_points: What God joined man must not separate.
The Kingdom belongs to children like these.
The rich young man went away grieved, owning much.
With God salvation is possible for the rich.
Those who left all will judge Israel’s twelve tribes in renewal.
evidence_phrases: What therefore God has joined together
the Kingdom of Heaven belongs to such as these
Why do you call me good?
sell all that you have
It is easier for a camel to go through a needle’s eye
With God all things are possible
many who are first will be last

title: Vineyard laborers, cup of suffering, and Bartimaeus
summary_text: The vineyard parable pays late workers the same as early, teaching God’s generosity and reversing grumbling about fairness. Jesus again foretells his suffering in Jerusalem and asks if disciples can drink his cup; they say yes yet greatness is service not ruling. Two blind men by the road cry for mercy; Jesus heals them by faith as the crowd had rebuked them.
themes: Son of Man to be mocked and killed
key_points: The master asked, is it not lawful to do what I want with my own?
Jesus predicted mockery, scourging, crucifixion, and resurrection on the third day.
The Son of Man came to serve and give his life a ransom.
The blind called Jesus Son of David and were healed by faith.
evidence_phrases: Didn’t you agree with me for a denarius?
the last will be first
the Son of Man will be delivered to the chief priests
Can you drink the cup that I am about to drink?
whoever desires to be first among you shall be your servant
Lord, Son of David, have mercy on us!

title: Entry, temple, fig tree, and parables of the tenants
summary_text: Jesus enters Jerusalem on a donkey with Hosanna cries; he drives money-changers from the temple and heals the blind and lame there. The cursed fig tree withers; he teaches mountain-moving faith and authority-questioning answers. The two sons and wicked tenants parables expose rejection of John and the Son; the stone the builders rejected becomes the cornerstone; the Kingdom is taken from Israel’s leaders and given to a nation producing fruit.
key_points: Tell the daughter of Zion her king comes humble on a donkey.
My house shall be called a house of prayer.
The fig tree withered overnight after Jesus cursed it.
Tax collectors and prostitutes enter the Kingdom before religious leaders.
The rejected stone has become the head of the corner.
evidence_phrases: Hosanna to the son of David!
My house shall be called a house of prayer
if you have faith, and don’t doubt
tax collectors and the prostitutes are entering into God’s Kingdom
The stone which the builders rejected
God’s Kingdom will be taken away from you

title: Wedding feast, taxes, resurrection, and the great commandment
summary_text: The wedding feast parable shows invited guests rejecting the king’s son; the hall is filled with good and bad for final examination. Pharisees and Herodians test the tribute coin; Jesus gives Caesar’s and God’s. Sadducees lose the resurrection argument about seven brothers’ wife. A lawyer’s question draws love for God and neighbor as the great commandment; Jesus then asks how David’s son is Lord, silencing the Pharisees.
key_points: The man without a wedding garment was cast out.
Give Caesar what is Caesar’s and God what is God’s.
God is not God of the dead but of the living.
The great commandment sums law and prophets.
David calls his son Lord—how then is he his son?
evidence_phrases: many are called, but few chosen
Render therefore to Caesar
He is not the God of the dead, but of the living
You shall love the Lord your God
You shall love your neighbor as yourself
David in Spirit called him Lord

title: Woes on the scribes and Pharisees
summary_text: Jesus warns the crowds and disciples not to imitate scribes and Pharisees who bind heavy burdens, love titles and places of honor, devour widows’ houses, and make proselytes twice children of hell. Seven woes expose hypocrisy: shut Kingdom, convert to child of Gehenna, blind guides on oaths, tithe neglecting justice, whitewashed tombs, serpent brood who kill prophets. He laments Jerusalem who kills prophets and stoners sent to her, unwilling under his wings as a hen gathers chicks.
key_points: Do not be called Rabbi, Father, or teacher—one is your teacher, Christ.
They strain gnats and swallow camels.
Inside they are full of extortion and self-indulgence.
You build tombs of prophets your fathers killed.
Your house is left to you desolate until you say blessed is he who comes.
evidence_phrases: they bind heavy burdens
All their works they do to be seen by men
woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites!
You blind Pharisee
You serpents, you offspring of vipers
Jerusalem, Jerusalem, who kills the prophets

title: Olivet discourse: signs and the coming Son of Man
summary_text: Jesus foretells temple destruction; disciples ask when and the sign of his coming and the age’s end. He warns of false christs, wars, famines, earthquakes, hatred, lawlessness, and gospel preached worldwide as birth pains. The abomination of desolation triggers Judean flight; those days are shortened for the elect’s sake. After tribulation the Son of Man appears with cosmic signs and gathers elect by angels. The fig tree lesson and Noah comparison urge watchfulness; the day and hour are unknown, so stay ready as faithful servants.
key_points: Not one stone here will be left on another.
Many will come in Jesus’s name deceiving.
The gospel will be preached in the whole world as witness.
Let those in Judea flee to the mountains when they see the abomination.
The Son of Man will come on clouds with power and great glory.
No one knows the day or hour except the Father.
evidence_phrases: not one stone here will be left on another
many will come in my name
this Good News of the Kingdom will be preached
when you see the abomination of desolation
they will see the Son of Man coming on the clouds
no one knows the day and hour
you also must be ready

title: Virgins, talents, and sheep and goats
summary_text: The kingdom of heaven is like ten virgins: five wise with oil meet the bridegroom; five foolish are shut out. Talents are entrusted; faithful servants multiply and enter joy; the one-talent servant is cast out. The Son of Man judges nations as shepherd separating sheep who served the least of his brothers from goats who did not, with eternal life or punishment.
key_points: The bridegroom came at midnight; the foolish had no oil.
Well done, good and faithful servant—enter into the joy of your lord.
The wicked servant was cast into outer darkness.
Inasmuch as you did it to one of the least of these, you did it to me.
These will go away into eternal punishment; the righteous into eternal life.
evidence_phrases: the bridegroom came
Lord, Lord, open to us
Well done, good and faithful servant
you wicked and slothful servant
Inasmuch as you did it to one of the least of these
Depart from me, you cursed
these will go away into eternal punishment

title: Last supper, Gethsemane, and trial
summary_text: The chief priests plot to seize Jesus by stealth; a woman anoints him for burial amid disciple objection. Judas agrees to betray for silver. Jesus eats Passover, institutes the cup and bread as his blood and body, predicts betrayal and denial, and prays in Gethsemane if the cup may pass yet not his will but the Father’s. Judas betrays with a kiss; disciples flee; trials before Caiaphas and Pilate follow with Peter’s denials and Judas’s remorse.
key_points: She has done a good work for me in preparing for my burial.
This is my blood of the new covenant shed for many.
Jesus knew who would betray him.
He prayed three times in Gethsemane for the Father’s will.
Peter denied Jesus thrice before the rooster crowed.
evidence_phrases: she has done a good work for me
one of you will betray me
this is my blood of the new covenant
not as I will, but as you will
Judas, do you betray the Son of Man with a kiss?
Before a rooster crows, you will deny me three times

title: Crucifixion and burial
summary_text: Judas returns silver; Jesus is delivered to Pilate; Judas hangs. Pilate’s wife warns him; the crowd chooses Barabbas and cries crucifixion. Soldiers mock, scourge, and crucify Jesus between thieves with a divided garment cast lots; darkness covers the land; Jesus cries forsaken and yields his spirit; the veil tears, earth quakes, saints raised. The centurion confesses God’s Son; women watch; Joseph buries Jesus in a new tomb sealed by Pilate’s guard.
key_points: Judas confessed betraying innocent blood.
Pilate washed his hands; the crowd took responsibility.
They crucified him and parted his garments.
From the sixth hour darkness until the ninth.
The temple veil tore top to bottom.
Joseph wrapped Jesus in clean linen in his own new tomb.
evidence_phrases: I have sinned in that I have betrayed innocent blood
His blood be on us, and on our children
They crucified him
My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?
Jesus cried out again with a loud voice, and yielded up his spirit
the veil of the temple was torn in two
Truly this was the Son of God

title: Resurrection and Great Commission
summary_text: Mary Magdalene and the other Mary find the empty tomb as the angel announces Jesus has risen; they meet Jesus who tells them not to fear but go tell the disciples he goes before them to Galilee. The guard report is bribed as risen rumor spreads. On the mountain Jesus claims all authority in heaven and earth, commands baptizing and teaching obedience in every nation, and promises presence to the world’s end.
key_points: The angel rolled back the stone and sat on it.
Jesus had risen as he said.
The women clasped his feet and worshiped.
Some doubted among the eleven in Galilee.
Baptize in the name of the Father, Son, and Holy Spirit.
I am with you always, even to the end of the age.
evidence_phrases: He is not here, for he has risen
Don’t be afraid
All authority has been given to me in heaven and on earth
make disciples of all nations
I am with you always, even to the end of the age
